hero.sub: Type a keyword. Pick your site. Graphref drives real search visits to your listing — so you don't have to wait months to move up.
hero.freeCredits: New users get 50 free credits — no card required.

howItWorks.title: How it works
howItWorks.step1Desc: No account setup. No forms. Just hit Start.
howItWorks.step3Desc: It drives real search visits to your site. You'll see it in Search Console.

faq.title: FAQ
faq.cantFind: Can't find what you're looking for? We're happy to help.

items.a: Graphref uses a network of real devices to perform genuine Google searches for your target keyword, then clicks through to your site from the search results. This simulates organic search behavior — the kind of engagement signal that Google uses to evaluate how relevant your page is to a given query. Because every visit originates from an actual search, it shows up as organic traffic in Google Search Console.

items.q: Will I see results in Google Search Console?
items.a: Yes. Since visits are driven through real search queries, they register as organic impressions and clicks in GSC. Most users start seeing movement within 24–72 hours of their first run. We recommend monitoring your target keyword's impression and click data in the Performance tab.

items.q: Is it safe? Will Google penalize my site?
items.a: Graphref is designed to mimic natural user behavior as closely as possible — real devices, real searches, realistic session patterns. We don't use bots or headless browsers. That said, no third-party traffic tool can guarantee zero risk, and we encourage you to use it as one part of a broader SEO strategy rather than a sole growth channel.

items.q: How many visits does one credit give me?
items.a: One run costs 10 credits and delivers 10 targeted search visits to your site. You choose the keyword and domain each time you run a job. There's no minimum spend — you can run a single job with your free credits to see how it works before purchasing more.

items.q: Can I target multiple keywords or domains?
items.a: Yes. Each /run command accepts one keyword and one domain, but you can run as many jobs as your credits allow — across different keywords, different pages, or entirely different sites. There's no restriction on how many domains you use.
Never. Credits you purchase are yours indefinitely. There's no monthly renewal, no expiry date, and no pressure to use them by a certain time. Buy when you need them, use them at your own pace.

items.q: What if a job doesn't complete successfully?
items.a: If a job fails for any reason — network issues, invalid domain, or a processing error on our end — your credits are automatically refunded in full. You'll receive a notification in the Telegram bot with the reason and your restored balance.

items.q: Is there a subscription or recurring charge?
items.a: No. Graphref is entirely credit-based. You pay once for a credit package and use them whenever you want. There's no subscription to manage, no auto-renewal to worry about, and nothing to cancel.

cta.title: Ready to try it?
cta.sub: Open the bot and run your first job in under a minute.

mission.line: Make search visibility accessible to people who ship.

hero.sub: So we built a tool that sends it instead. Graphref is a small team obsessed with one problem: making search visibility accessible to people who ship things.

founder.title: Founder / Team
founder.whyBuiltLine1: Graphref started because builders shouldn't wait months just to be seen in search.
founder.whyBuiltLine2: We're a lean team running product, infrastructure, and growth experiments as one loop.

founder.label: Founder
founder.name: Sngmin
founder.bio: Owns product direction end-to-end, from shipping core features to designing ranking signal experiments.

team.label: Team
team.name: Graphref Core Team
team.bio: A small cross-functional team that ships fast and turns user feedback into product updates immediately.

story.block1p1: We ran sites. We wrote content. We built backlinks. Google still took months to notice. Meanwhile, big players with established authority kept ranking — not because their content was better, but because they had history.
story.block1p2: We asked: what if we could simulate the signal Google actually cares about — real people searching, finding your site, clicking through?
story.block2Title: A traffic engine, not an SEO tool
story.block2p1: Graphref simulates the click signal — searches for your keyword, clicks to your listing. It's artificial by design. But so is every other tool that tries to influence ranking.
story.block2p2: No plugins, no code, no site access needed. Telegram-first because nobody wants another dashboard. You see the signal in Search Console within hours.

timeline.2023body: Running sites, writing content, building backlinks. Months of work. Google didn't notice.
timeline.2024body: Realized search signals matter more than content. Real clicks from real searches move rankings.
timeline.2025body: Built a tool to simulate organic visits. Tested it. It worked. Search Console moved within hours.
timeline.2026body: Made it Telegram-first. Credits, not subscriptions. Now powering 3,800+ sites worldwide.

items.title: Simplicity is the feature.
items.body: You shouldn't need a tutorial to send traffic to your site. One command. One result. That's the whole product.

items.title: Transparency over hype.
items.body: We don't promise #1 rankings. Click signals sit in a grey area with Google's guidelines — we say that clearly. Test small, watch Search Console, make your own call.

items.title: Built for builders.
items.body: Indie hackers, solo founders, small agencies. People who ship fast and need results faster. Not enterprise sales cycles.

items.title: Credits, not subscriptions.
items.body: Your money shouldn't evaporate every month. Credits don't expire. Use them when you need them.

items.title: We don't pretend this is risk-free.
items.body: Injecting click signals is a grey area. Google may detect patterns over time. We say this upfront. Start with 50 free credits, watch Search Console, and decide if it's worth it for your situation.

sponsor.p1: Graphref is an indie project. If you're in SEO, run a newsletter, or build tools for founders — we're open to partnerships that make sense for both sides.
sponsor.p2: No pitch decks. Just reach out.

cta.sub: Watch Search Console. If it moves, buy more. If it doesn't, you lost nothing.

contact.title: Contact
contact.sub: Have a question or need help? We'll get back to you within 1–2 business days.
contact.faqHint: Most answers are in the FAQ.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: These Terms of Service govern your access to and use of Graphref, including the website, Telegram bot, checkout flows, credits, and related support channels. By accessing or using Graphref, you agree to be bound by these Terms. If you do not agree, do not use the Service.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: This Privacy Policy explains how Graphref collects, uses, stores, discloses, and protects personal information when you use our website, Telegram bot, checkout flow, and related support channels. It is meant to describe our actual operating practices in a clear, legal-policy format. If you do not agree with this Policy, do not use the Service.
